timeframe: 5m/15m intraday bars, one decision per RTH session
data_source: TradingView OHLCV via the DT Intraday Momentum v1 Pine indicator/strategy pair, cross-checked against the source academic papers (Gao-Han-Li-Zhou JFE 1993-2013; Baltussen et al. 1974-2020 global futures extension) for the unadapted logic; no external verification feed for the DT SL/TP1 overlay, which is untested by any source
entry: At the decision bar T (default 30 minutes before session close, e.g. 15:30 ET for a 16:00 ET close), compute daySoFarReturn = close(T) / session_open_price - 1.
If open30Return and daySoFarReturn are both non-zero and share the same sign, enter long when the sign is positive and short when the sign is negative, at the close of the decision bar T.
No entry if either return is exactly zero or the signs disagree.
Exactly one entry per session per instrument; no re-entry after the decision bar even if conditions would otherwise qualify.
exit_take_profit: TP1: close 50% of the position at 1.0x ATR(14) favorable move from entry (DT-contract overlay, not validated by any source study).
TP2 / remainder: hold the remaining 50% to a hard time exit at session close (matches the source studies' tested 'let it run to close' exit; there is no TP2 limit price).
exit_stop_loss: Protective disaster stop at 1.5x ATR(14) beyond entry, applied to the full position at entry and to the TP1 remainder until the EOD time exit (DT-contract overlay, not present in the source studies, which have no native stop-loss).
position_size: 10% of equity per trade (strategy.percent_of_equity, matching the DT ORB v1 reference convention); risk sized off the ATR-based SL distance, not a fixed dollar amount.

risk.max_risk_per_trade: 0.5% equity (position sized so 1.5x ATR(14) stop distance corresponds to at most 0.5% equity risk)
risk.daily_loss_limit: 1.0% equity; stop taking new signals for the remainder of the day after the daily loss limit is hit (moot in practice since only 1 signal fires per day, but applies across multiple concurrently-run instrument instances)
kill_switch: Live/paper P&L diverges materially from the ATR-overlay backtest for 10 consecutive sessions
TradingView CDP/data feed failure preventing decision-bar detection
Manual override required before any live-sizing promotion beyond paper trading

benchmark: Buy-and-hold ES continuous futures (or SPY) over the same dates

validation.negative_result_log: Preserve the parameters and metrics for each failed, negative, rejected, or underperforming variant tested locally (e.g. alternate ATR multiples for SL/TP1, alternate decision-time offsets, SPY-ETF vs. index-futures instrument choice) in journal/backtests/ per variant/instrument/timeframe run, not only in aggregate.
validation.diagnostic_test: Before refining any locally-failing variant, record a counter-thesis for the failure cause (e.g. 'SL/TP1 overlay is cutting the run-to-close tail that produces the edge'), then run one one-variable ablation test (e.g. remove the TP1 partial exit and compare only-EOD-exit performance against the same signal set) and record the observed, actual difference in the outcome metric.
validation.point_in_time_data: Single-instrument system: n/a for universe membership. OHLCV bars are available at the decision time T, matching process_orders_on_close=true.
validation.parameter_freeze: Freeze the SL/TP1 ATR multiples, opening-window length, and decision-time offset before opening the 2025-01-01..2026-06-30 holdout window; no holdout-driven retuning of any parameter after the freeze.

execution.primary_fill_model: Fill at the close of the decision bar T (process_orders_on_close=true, matching the DT ORB v1 reference convention); EOD exit fills at the close of the last in-session bar.
execution.stress_fill_model: Conservative one-bar-delayed fill at the following bar open, to approximate slippage beyond the decision-bar close.
execution.commission: 0.005% per side primary (matching the DT ORB v1 reference strategy header) and 0.02% per side stress.
execution.spread: 1 tick primary (ES-class index futures) or 1 cent (SPY-class ETF) and 3 ticks / 3 cents adverse stress.
execution.slippage: 2 ticks primary (matching the strategy header's slippage=2) and 5 ticks adverse stress.
execution.leverage_funding: 1x gross leverage for the ETF variant; standard index-futures margin (no additional leverage beyond exchange initial margin) for the futures variant. Funding 0 bps for the cash-ETF variant; futures funding is embedded in the roll/basis and is not separately modeled.

robustness.top_trade_removal: Recompute annualized return and Sharpe after removing the single largest winning trade, and again after removing the top 5% of winning trades, per instrument.
regime_splits: trend/range (ADX-based classification of the RTH session)
high/low volatility (VIX or realized-vol tercile split)
COVID-crash subperiod vs. non-crash subperiod (the source paper's OOS retest found the edge concentrated in the COVID period, Sharpe 1.452 vs. -0.628 overall)
robustness.long_short_decomposition: Report long and short trade counts, win rates, and average R separately for both the futures and ETF variants; this setup is not long-only so both sides must be reported.
robustness.concentration_check: Report the top 1, top 3, and top 5 winning-day PnL contribution as a percentage of total strategy PnL, with a concentration cap of 40% for the top 5 winning days fixed before the holdout period; given the low OOS R² (1.2-2.8%) documented in the source research, high concentration in a small number of days is expected and must be disclosed, not treated as an anomaly.

review_cadence: weekly paper review; monthly parameter review
edge_death_condition: Live/paper P&L diverges from the ATR-overlay backtest for 10 consecutive sessions
Realized OOS Sharpe falls below -1.0 for either the futures or ETF variant, echoing the documented 2010-2018 SPY negative replication (Sharpe -2.70)
Regime mismatch: edge only present during extreme-volatility periods (e.g. only positive during a COVID-like crash subperiod) rather than persisting across the full holdout

human_confirmation.live_orders: manual only; this spec checker never places orders. This setup is a research candidate and must not be marked 'adopted' in journal/registry.json until the holdout period above is completed and independently reviewed.
